Product Name: H-4218 - Liposomal NAD+ Cellular Renewal Drops
H4213 - Cell Peak NMNH Longevity Complex
Listing Notes:
NMNH is blowing up. It’s already at a scale comparable to NAD+. However, for a keyword this large in search volume, competitors are underperforming, and demand is not translating into the level of sales you would expect. This keyword also started gaining momentum around 3 months ago which leaves plenty of room for new sellers to enter and get their sales ramping up, especially since the average capsule supplement lead time is roughly 2–3 months as well. But this wasn’t the case here.
My best guess is that consumers are searching for a reduced form of NMN -> NMNH. But, they end up purchasing NMN supplements instead because they are much more well-positioned than NMNH. This is why the better opportunity right now remains NAD+ and NMN, not NMNH.

We will need to drop this product. Magnesium sulphate and magnesium chloride are the only forms that can be spray-coated. Magnesium sulphate functions as a laxative, while magnesium chloride is primarily used as a bath salt and is not ideal for a food product. Magnesium citrate from TopGUM is also not viable, as citrate is not GRAS for use in conventional food products. This also means that we won’t be able to use Magnesium for any of our spray-coated gummy SKUs in future.

The keyword spike is coming from kava’s rapid adoption as an alcohol alternative. Millennials and Gen Z are actively replacing alcohol with functional drinks, and kava is being consumed primarily in ready-to-drink and bar-style formats, not as a traditional supplement like drops, capsules, powders, or gummies. That beverage-driven shift is what’s driving the search volume increase.

Did a quick dive in the niche again. This space is extremely concentrated now, with just 2 brands (Nello and Nature Vitality) controlling over 90% of the market share. Leading with a Magnesium Calm Powder positioning would be a dead end. Additionally, our current “Yerba Mate” differentiator isn’t strong enough to realistically capture meaningful share in such a dominated market.

Product Name: HL107 - Oil Of oregano + Black seed oil
Listing Notes:
:white_check_mark: ASIN B0GTRJVHQB
:white_check_mark: UPC 850076186869
R&D Recommendation:
:hourglass_flowing_sand: *Changes Required.* I originally rejected this product for lack of differentiation, but because we already have the raw materials ordered, we’re moving forward with the formula.

I would add *Moringa, Soursop, and Chlorophyll* on top of our existing formula if you want to stay differentiated. Why?

• All three have high standalone search volume on Amazon.
• A Soursop-led drop doing 7k+ sales/mo already uses our exact core stack (Oregano Oil, Turmeric, Black Seed Oil) alongside Chlorophyll and Moringa, so the combo is proven already.
• The gap: this trio exists in Soursop-led drops, but nobody is running it in Oregano Oil-led drops yet

*The catch:* all three are water-soluble. Won’t work in our current EVOO oil base. We’d need to switch to a water/glycerin base (same approach as Clean Nutra’s Oregano Oil drops).

*For record,* Clean Nutra does include Soursop with their Oregano drops, but it’s buried in their SFP with zero mention on their title or packaging. We’d own it on the bottle front.

*The trade-off:* switching from oil base (0.5ml serving) to water/glycerin (2ml serving) drops our servings per container from 120 to 30. We lose the high serving count that other Oregano competitors offer, but gain a stronger formula with more keyword reach.

To conclude, we have two options:

*Option A:* Go with high servings (using an oil base), but keep the existing, undifferentiated formula.

*Option B:* Go with a differentiated formula (with a water base), but with 30 servings per bottle only.

*My take:* I would go with Option B since water-base Oregano drops still work and Clean Nutra has proven it.

:white_check_mark: *Proceed*
• We have to do a GLP-1 Multi-Vitamin Capsule as a variation alongside our core GLP-1 Akkermansia Capsules. Nobody's doing this right now. I think our CVR would be solid compared to competitors who are just running 60ct, 120ct, 180ct as their variations.
• Also, demand for GLP-1 vitamins has 3x'd since I last looked, we're at 30k+ monthly SV now (attached). Trend keeps climbing like we expected (graph attached as well).
• Added this to the IDEAS list. Will dig into it more once I'm through validating everything else on the list.

R&D Recommendation:
:hourglass_flowing_sand: *Changes Required.* Looks like the formula got updated since I last touched it. We brought the liver blend down from 100mg to 40mg. Here are a few tweaks I'd make:

• Pull Glutathione out. At 10mg buried in a blend, it's basically pixie dust. But honestly, that's not even the main reason for its removal. NAC at 200mg is already the body's go-to precursor for making glutathione, so having both is redundant. We don't lose any keyword value as well since you can still use "glutathione" search terms in the bullets due to the inclusion of NAC.
• Bump Dandelion up to 100mg as a hero. Dandelion root is a strong keyword ingredient in the liver/detox space, and hiding it at 10mg in a blend is a waste. 
• Drop DHM from 300mg → 200mg to make room for the dandelion move above. 200mg is still a solid dose for alcohol metabolism support, so we're not losing anything here.
• Add Ginger + Artichoke to the blend. Ginger makes total sense for the after-drinking use case (nausea, gut stuff), and artichoke supports bile flow and liver function. Neither one is expensive either.
